questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the following data (all values in $\mathrm{kJ} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ ) and answer the questions that follow. (i) Enthalpy of polymerisation of ethene $=-72$ (ii) Enthalpy of formation of benzene (I) $=49$ (iii) Enthalpy of vaporisation of benzene ( I$)=30$ (iv) Resonance energy of benzene $=-152$ (v) Heat of formation of gaseous atoms from the element in their standard state: $\mathrm{H}=218, \mathrm{C}=715$ and average $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}$ bond energy $=415$Question: Bond enthalpy of $\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}$ bond is
questions.solution:
Let B.E. of $C=C$ be $x$ and $B . E$. of $C-C$ be $y$ $\mathrm{n}\left(\mathrm{CH}_2=\mathrm{CH}_2\right) \rightarrow\left(\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2^{-}\right)_{\mathrm{n}}$ $\Rightarrow x-2 y=-72$ ...(i)
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta H_r=\Delta H_f\left(C_6 H_6, I\right)+\Delta H_{\text {vap }}\left(C_6 H_6, I\right)-R E \\ & =49+30-(-152)=231 \\ & 231=6 \times 715+6 \times 218-3(x+y)-6 \times 415\end{aligned}$ $x+y=959$ On solving (i) and (ii) we get $x=615.33$ and $y=343.67$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Enthalpy change for a chemical reaction can be calculated from bond enthalpy data. Bond enthalpy depends upon structural environment of the bond. From the data given below, answer the questions that follow. $\Delta \mathrm{H}_t\left[\mathrm{PCl}_3(\mathrm{~g})\right]=-320 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{t}}[\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{g})]=315 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and $\Delta \mathrm{H}_t\left[\mathrm{PCl}_5(\mathrm{~g})\right]=-400 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$Question: Calculate bond enthalpy of $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{Cl}$ bond in $\mathrm{PCl}_3$ if enthalpy of atomisation of $\mathrm{Cl}_2(\mathrm{~g})=240 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the following data (all values in $\mathrm{kJ} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ ) and answer the questions that follow. (i) Enthalpy of polymerisation of ethene $=-72$ (ii) Enthalpy of formation of benzene (I) $=49$ (iii) Enthalpy of vaporisation of benzene ( I$)=30$ (iv) Resonance energy of benzene $=-152$ (v) Heat of formation of gaseous atoms from the element in their standard state: $\mathrm{H}=218, \mathrm{C}=715$ and average $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}$ bond energy $=415$Question: Difference between bond enthalpy of $\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}$ and $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C}$ bond is
questions.solution:
Let B.E. of $C=C$ be $x$ and $B . E$. of $C-C$ be $y$ $\mathrm{n}\left(\mathrm{CH}_2=\mathrm{CH}_2\right) \rightarrow\left(\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2^{-}\right)_{\mathrm{n}}$ $\Rightarrow x-2 y=-72$ ...(i)
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta H_r=\Delta H_f\left(C_6 H_6, I\right)+\Delta H_{\text {vap }}\left(C_6 H_6, I\right)-R E \\ & =49+30-(-152)=231 \\ & 231=6 \times 715+6 \times 218-3(x+y)-6 \times 415\end{aligned}$ $x+y=959$ On solving (i) and (ii) we get $x=615.33$ and $y=343.67$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph A monoatomic ideal gas undergoes state change from initial state $(A)$ to final state $(E)$ through two paths. $\mathbf{A} \rightarrow \mathbf{B}$ and $\mathrm{D} \rightarrow \mathrm{E}$ are isothermal; $\mathrm{B} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}$ and $\mathrm{C} \rightarrow \mathrm{D}$ are adiabatic processes
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Work }=|60| \mathrm{J} \text { from } A \rightarrow B \text {, work }=|50| J \text { from } B \rightarrow C \\ & \text { Work }=|40| J \text { from } C \rightarrow D \text {, work }=|35| J \text { from } D \rightarrow E\end{aligned}$Question $\Delta S$ and $\Delta H$ for the above state change from $A$ to $E$ are
questions.solution: $\Delta S$ is a state function, so independent of path. $\Delta S$ is zero in reversible adiabatic path $\Delta \mathrm{H}$ is zero in isothermal process.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Paragraph: Enthalpy change for a chemical reaction can be calculated from bond enthalpy data. Bond enthalpy depends upon structural environment of the bond. From the data given below, answer the questions that follow. $\Delta \mathrm{H}_t\left[\mathrm{PCl}_3(\mathrm{~g})\right]=-320 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{t}}[\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{g})]=315 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and $\Delta \mathrm{H}_t\left[\mathrm{PCl}_5(\mathrm{~g})\right]=-400 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$Question: Calculate enthalpy of formation of $\mathbf{C}\left(\right.$ diamond) if enthalpy of atomisation of $\mathbf{C}$ (graphite) $=710 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and energy required to break $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C}$ bond in diamond (solid) $=354.05 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Concrete is produced from a mixture of cement, water, sand and small stones. It consists primarily of calcium silicates and calcium aluminates formed by heating and grinding of clay and limestone. In later steps of cement production a small amount of gypsum, $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is added to improve subsequent hardening of concrete. The use of elevated temperatures during the final production may lead to formation of unwanted hemihydrate, $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 1 / 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. Consider the following reaction: $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s})+\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g})$ The following thermodynamic data apply at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, standard pressure: 1 bar
Question: $\Delta \mathrm{H}^{\circ}$ for the formation of 1.00 kg of $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s})$ from $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s})$ is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Paragraph: Concrete is produced from a mixture of cement, water, sand and small stones. It consists primarily of calcium silicates and calcium aluminates formed by heating and grinding of clay and limestone. In later steps of cement production a small amount of gypsum, $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is added to improve subsequent hardening of concrete. The use of elevated temperatures during the final production may lead to formation of unwanted hemihydrate, $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 1 / 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. Consider the following reaction: $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s})+\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g})$ The following thermodynamic data apply at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, standard pressure: 1 bar
Question: Equilibrium pressure (in bar) of water vapour in closed vessel containing $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ (s), $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s}), \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g})$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$

questions.q:
Paragraph: Concrete is produced from a mixture of cement, water, sand and small stones. It consists primarily of calcium silicates and calcium aluminates formed by heating and grinding of clay and limestone. In later steps of cement production a small amount of gypsum, $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is added to improve subsequent hardening of concrete. The use of elevated temperatures during the final production may lead to formation of unwanted hemihydrate, $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 1 / 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. Consider the following reaction: $\mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{s})+\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{g})$ The following thermodynamic data apply at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, standard pressure: 1 bar
Question: Temperature at which the equilibrium water vapour pressure is 1.00 bar

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph A monoatomic ideal gas undergoes state change from initial state $(A)$ to final state $(E)$ through two paths. $\mathbf{A} \rightarrow \mathbf{B}$ and $\mathrm{D} \rightarrow \mathrm{E}$ are isothermal; $\mathrm{B} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}$ and $\mathrm{C} \rightarrow \mathrm{D}$ are adiabatic processes
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Work }=|60| \mathrm{J} \text { from } A \rightarrow B \text {, work }=|50| J \text { from } B \rightarrow C \\ & \text { Work }=|40| J \text { from } C \rightarrow D \text {, work }=|35| J \text { from } D \rightarrow E\end{aligned}$Question Select the correct statement about internal energy or work done for the above state change from A to E
options: The magnitude of work done by the gas from $A$ to $C$ is equal to heat gained by the gas from $C$ to $E$
questions.solution:
$\Delta E_A \rightarrow_B$ and $\Delta E_D \rightarrow_E$ are zero because $\Delta T=0$
$\left(\Delta E_{\text {Path-1 }}=(\Delta E)_{\text {Patt-2 }}=\Delta E_B \rightarrow c+\Delta E_c \rightarrow 0\right.$
$B \rightarrow C$ and $C \rightarrow D$ are adiabatic so $\Delta E=w$
$(\Delta \mathrm{E})_{\text {Path }-2}=-50+35=-15 \mathrm{~J}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
